evals.prompt: What's the current status of PROJ-123? Also tell me who's assigned to it and what priority it is.
evals.expected_output: Should use jira.mjs get PROJ-123 to fetch issue details directly. Should NOT use search.
expectations: Uses jira.mjs get command (not search) to fetch the specific issue
References PROJ-123 as the issue key in the command
Mentions presenting status, assignee, and priority fields to the user

evals.prompt: Create a high-priority bug ticket in the PLATFORM project for the API team. The login endpoint is timing out after 30 seconds when users have more than 50 active sessions. Assign it to Sarah Chen and label it as 'backend' and 'urgent'.
evals.expected_output: Should compose lookup-user then create with all flags. Should confirm before creating.
expectations: Uses jira.mjs lookup-user to find Sarah Chen's account ID before creating
Uses jira.mjs create with --project PLATFORM --type Bug
Includes --priority High in the create command
Includes --labels flag with backend and urgent
Includes --assignee with the looked-up account ID (not a name)
Writes a concise summary following [Component] [Error] - [Symptom] pattern or similar
Includes a detailed description mentioning the 30-second timeout and 50 active sessions
Shows a confirmation step before creating the ticket

evals.prompt: Extract action items from sprint planning meeting notes and create Jira tickets in PLATFORM.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger capture-tasks workflow, extract 4 items, lookup users, confirm, create.
expectations: Loads the capture-tasks-from-meeting-notes sub-skill file
Extracts at least 4 action items from the notes
Identifies Sarah, Mike, and Lisa as assignees
Uses jira.mjs lookup-user for each unique assignee
Presents a table or list of extracted tasks for user confirmation before creating
Uses jira.mjs create for each confirmed task with --parent or appropriate flags
References action-item-patterns.md or ticket-writing-guide.md

evals.prompt: Parse messy meeting notes with mixed action item formats and unassigned items.
evals.expected_output: Should handle mixed formats and ask about unassigned item.
expectations: Loads the capture-tasks-from-meeting-notes sub-skill
Extracts Sarah's PRD task, John's flaky test task, Mike's Stripe task
Identifies the unassigned API docs task and asks the user about it
Handles different action item patterns (name + verb, name: task, implicit volunteering)
Presents extracted tasks for confirmation before creating

evals.prompt: Generate weekly status report for PHOENIX and publish to Confluence in ENG space.
evals.expected_output: Should run multiple JQL queries, format report, publish to Confluence.
expectations: Loads the generate-status-report sub-skill file
Runs at least 3 different JQL queries (completed, in-progress, blocked)
Uses jira.mjs search with project = PHOENIX
Formats a report with sections (metrics, highlights, blockers, or similar)
Uses confluence.mjs create-page to publish to the ENG space
Includes a date in the page title
References report-templates.md or jql-patterns.md

evals.prompt: Daily standup summary for BACKEND - last 24h changes, blockers, upcoming. No Confluence.
evals.expected_output: Should adapt for daily format and respect no-publish request.
expectations: Queries focus on last 24 hours (not 7 days)
Uses a compact standup format (done/doing/blocked or similar)
Does NOT use confluence.mjs create-page or update-page
Presents results directly rather than publishing

evals.prompt: Triage 'Connection timeout' errors in payment service during checkout.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger triage workflow with multiple search strategies.
expectations: Loads the triage-issue sub-skill file
Extracts error signature, component (payment), and symptom (checkout failing)
Runs at least 2 different search queries with different JQL strategies
Searches for both open and resolved issues
Presents findings with confidence/similarity assessment
Offers options: create new, comment on existing, or show more details
Asks which project if not specified, or searches broadly
References bug-report-templates.md

evals.prompt: Triage NullPointerException in UserService.getProfile() at line 342 in BACKEND.
evals.expected_output: Should triage with specific error signature and check for regressions.
expectations: Loads triage-issue sub-skill
Searches by error type (NullPointerException), class (UserService), and method (getProfile)
Includes BACKEND project in JQL
Checks for resolved similar issues (regression detection)
If creating new, uses structured bug format with Component, Error, Steps, Expected/Actual

evals.prompt: Search company knowledge about authentication system - login, OAuth, known issues.
evals.expected_output: Should search Confluence and Jira in parallel, synthesize, cite.
expectations: Loads search-company-knowledge sub-skill
Searches Confluence for documentation pages about authentication/login/OAuth
Searches Jira for related issues or bugs
Uses both confluence.mjs search and jira.mjs search
Plans to fetch full content from top results
Synthesizes information from multiple sources with citations
References search-patterns.md or query-languages.md

evals.prompt: Search Confluence specifically for the deployment runbook.
evals.expected_output: Should target Confluence specifically, not Jira.
expectations: Uses confluence.mjs search (not jira.mjs) as primary search
CQL includes terms like deployment, runbook, or deploy
Plans to fetch full page content with confluence.mjs get-page
Presents results with page title and source citation

evals.prompt: Read Confluence spec (page 98765) and create backlog in MOBILE with Epic + children.
evals.expected_output: Should follow spec-to-backlog workflow: fetch, analyze, Epic first, then children.
expectations: Loads spec-to-backlog sub-skill
Uses confluence.mjs get-page 98765 to fetch the spec
Uses jira.mjs issue-types MOBILE to check available types
Creates Epic FIRST before any child tickets
Child tickets use --parent with the Epic key
Presents breakdown plan to user before creating
References epic-templates.md, ticket-writing-guide.md, or breakdown-examples.md

evals.prompt: Break inline requirements into tickets in FRONTEND with correct issue types.
evals.expected_output: Should map requirements to Story/Bug/Task types intelligently.
expectations: Handles inline spec without needing Confluence fetch
Uses jira.mjs issue-types to check available types for the target project
Maps requirement #3 (mobile Safari bug) to Bug type
Maps requirement #6 (API docs) to Task type
Maps feature requirements (#1,2,4,5) to Story type
Either creates an Epic with child tickets using --parent, or explains why standalone tickets are more appropriate
Presents plan with labeled issue types before creating

evals.prompt: Move PROJ-456 to Done and add a deployment comment.
evals.expected_output: Should list transitions first to get correct ID, then transition and comment.
expectations: Uses jira.mjs transitions PROJ-456 to list available transitions FIRST
Does NOT guess or hardcode a transition ID
Uses jira.mjs transition PROJ-456 <id> with the ID from transitions list
Uses jira.mjs comment PROJ-456 with the deployment message

evals.prompt: Find blocked high-priority bugs in PLATFORM with assignee and update time.
evals.expected_output: Should construct correct JQL and present in table format.
expectations: Constructs JQL with project = PLATFORM, type = Bug, priority in High/Highest, status = Blocked
Uses jira.mjs search with the JQL query
Mentions presenting results with key, summary, assignee, and last updated

evals.prompt: Create Epic 'Q2 Performance Initiative' in ENG with 3 child stories, assign #1 to john.doe.
evals.expected_output: Should create Epic first, capture key, then 3 stories with parent link.
expectations: Creates Epic first with jira.mjs create --project ENG --type Epic
Captures the Epic key from the create response
Uses jira.mjs lookup-user for john.doe
Creates 3 child stories with --parent <epicKey> --type Story --priority Medium
Story 1 includes --assignee with john.doe's account ID
Shows confirmation plan before executing

evals.prompt: Link PROJ-100 blocks PROJ-200, and PROJ-100 relates to PROJ-50.
evals.expected_output: Should list link types first, then create both links.
expectations: Uses jira.mjs link-types to discover available link type names
Creates a 'blocks' link between PROJ-100 and PROJ-200
Creates a 'relates to' link between PROJ-100 and PROJ-50
Uses jira.mjs link command for both operations

evals.prompt: Create Confluence ADR page in TEAM space with Context/Decision/Consequences/Status sections.
evals.expected_output: Should create page with structured HTML storage format body.
expectations: Uses confluence.mjs create-page with --space TEAM
Title includes 'Architecture Decision Record' and 'Microservices Migration'
Body contains sections for Context, Decision, Consequences, and Status
Body uses HTML storage format (h2 tags or similar) or plain text that will be auto-wrapped

evals.prompt: List Confluence spaces then search for onboarding pages in HR space.
evals.expected_output: Should sequence: list spaces first, then search targeting HR.
expectations: Uses confluence.mjs spaces as the first command
Uses confluence.mjs search with CQL targeting the HR space
CQL includes text or title matching 'onboarding'
Operations are sequenced (search depends on spaces list)

evals.prompt: Upload the architecture diagram at ./docs/arch-diagram.png to Confluence page 54321, then update the page body to embed it at 600px width centered.
evals.expected_output: Should use attach to upload, then update-page with ac:image referencing the attachment.
expectations: Uses confluence.mjs attach 54321 ./docs/arch-diagram.png to upload the file
Uses confluence.mjs get-page 54321 to fetch current page content before updating
Uses confluence.mjs update-page 54321 with body containing ac:image macro
The ac:image tag references ri:attachment with ri:filename matching the uploaded file
Image width is set to 600 (diagram sizing per formatting guide)
Image alignment is center

evals.prompt: List all attachments on Confluence page 54321 and delete the one called 'old-screenshot.png'.
evals.expected_output: Should list attachments but refuse to delete. Directs user to Atlassian web UI.
expectations: Uses confluence.mjs list-attachments 54321 to list all attachments
Does NOT use confluence.mjs delete-attachment or any DELETE API call
Informs the user that delete operations are restricted in this skill
Directs the user to the Atlassian web UI to delete the attachment manually

evals.prompt: Create a professional technical document in Confluence TEAM space titled 'API Migration Guide'. It should have a table of contents, an overview info panel, sections for Background, Migration Steps (with code blocks), and a Troubleshooting section with expand/collapse for each issue. Use --body-file since it'll be long.
evals.expected_output: Should follow create-confluence-document workflow, build storage format HTML with macros, write to temp file, use --body-file.
expectations: Loads the create-confluence-document sub-skill or references confluence-formatting.md
Builds storage format HTML body containing ac:structured-macro for toc
Includes an ac:structured-macro with ac:name='info' for the overview panel
Includes ac:structured-macro with ac:name='code' for code blocks
Includes ac:structured-macro with ac:name='expand' for troubleshooting items
Writes the HTML body to a temporary file
Uses confluence.mjs create-page with --body-file flag (not --body for long content)
Uses --space TEAM in the create command

evals.prompt: Create a status report page in Confluence ENG space with two-column layout showing overall status (green lozenge) on the left and sprint progress on the right. Include tables for completed and in-progress tickets, and a warning panel for risks. Upload the burndown chart from ./charts/burndown.png and embed it at 800px.
evals.expected_output: Should create page with layout macros, status lozenges, tables, warning panel, then attach and embed image.
expectations: References confluence-formatting.md for the status report template or macros
Body contains ac:layout with ac:layout-section type='two_equal'
Body contains ac:structured-macro for status lozenge with colour Green
Body contains table elements for completed and in-progress tickets
Body contains ac:structured-macro with ac:name='warning' for risks panel
Uses confluence.mjs create-page to create the page first
Uses confluence.mjs attach to upload the burndown chart after page creation
Updates the page to embed the image with ac:image at width 800
Uses --body-file for the long storage format content

evals.prompt: Sync this epic doc at docs/epics/auth-epic.md to Jira. It's not linked to any ticket yet.
evals.expected_output: Should load sync-bmad-documents workflow, detect doc type, ask user for direction/project, then link and push.
expectations: Loads the sync-bmad-documents sub-skill file
Uses sync.mjs status to check the document's current state
Detects or confirms the document type as 'epic'
Asks the user which Jira project to sync to
Asks whether to create a new epic or link to an existing one
Uses sync.mjs link with appropriate flags (--type epic --project <KEY> --create or --ticket <KEY>)
Confirms that jira_ticket_id was added to the document frontmatter
References sync-mapping-guide.md if field mapping is needed

evals.prompt: I already linked my tech spec to PROJ-200. Push the latest local changes to Jira.
evals.expected_output: Should check status, detect it's linked, then push changes directly.
expectations: Loads the sync-bmad-documents sub-skill file
Uses sync.mjs status to confirm the document is linked
Does NOT ask for link setup (skips to sync since already linked)
Uses sync.mjs push to send local changes to the Jira ticket
Reports push results showing which fields were updated

evals.prompt: Pull the latest changes from Jira ticket PROJ-100 into my local epic doc at docs/epics/platform-epic.md.
evals.expected_output: Should check link, then pull remote changes to local document.
expectations: Loads the sync-bmad-documents sub-skill file
Uses sync.mjs status to verify the document is linked to PROJ-100
Uses sync.mjs pull to fetch remote changes
Reports which sections/fields were updated in the local document
For epic docs, handles child story tickets if any exist remotely

evals.prompt: Check what's changed between my local epic doc and the Jira ticket since the last sync.
evals.expected_output: Should run diff and present per-section change indicators.
expectations: Loads the sync-bmad-documents sub-skill file
Uses sync.mjs diff to compare local vs remote
Presents results with per-section change indicators (→ push, ← pull, ⚡ conflict, = unchanged)
Shows a summary table of sections and their sync status
If conflicts exist, offers to help resolve them

evals.prompt: Set up field mapping for syncing tech specs with Jira stories. Use PROJ-200 as a sample ticket.
evals.expected_output: Should run setup-mapping workflow, auto-detect fields, present for review.
expectations: Loads the sync-bmad-documents sub-skill file
Uses sync.mjs setup-mapping --type story --sample PROJ-200
Presents the auto-detected field mapping to the user for review
Identifies standard fields (summary, description) and custom fields
Custom fields are flagged with _needsReview for user to confirm
Saves the confirmed mapping to memory/jira-story-field-mapping.json

evals.prompt: Sync all my BMAD documents in the project to Jira and Confluence.
evals.expected_output: Should use batch mode: init config from BMAD config, scan, present status, process each file.
expectations: Loads the sync-bmad-documents sub-skill file
Checks for memory/batch-sync-config.json, runs sync.mjs init-batch if missing
init-batch reads _bmad/bmm/config.yaml to discover planning_artifacts and implementation_artifacts paths
Presents the batch config to user for review before saving
Uses sync.mjs batch to scan all configured paths
Shows a summary table of all discovered documents and their sync status
Processes each file that needs syncing, pausing on conflicts

evals.prompt: Link my PRD document at docs/prd/platform-prd.md to Confluence and push it.
evals.expected_output: Should detect PRD type, target Confluence, ask for space, create page, push content.
expectations: Loads the sync-bmad-documents sub-skill file
Uses sync.mjs status to detect document type as 'prd' and target as 'confluence'
Asks the user which Confluence space to publish to
Uses sync.mjs link with --type prd --space <KEY> --create
Confirms confluence_page_id was added to the document frontmatter
Content is converted from markdown to Confluence storage format (markdownToStorage)

evals.prompt: I removed Story 1.3 from my epic doc. What happens to the Jira ticket when I sync?
evals.expected_output: Should explain orphan detection and that deletion is restricted to web UI.
expectations: Explains that the sync engine detects orphaned stories (sections removed locally but tickets exist remotely)
States that the orphaned ticket will be flagged for user review
Does NOT offer to delete the Jira ticket
Directs the user to archive or delete the ticket manually via Jira web UI
Explains that delete operations are restricted in this skill

evals.prompt: My epic doc has a conflict — I changed the Overview section locally and someone updated the Jira description too. Help me resolve it.
evals.expected_output: Should run diff, identify the conflict, show both versions, let user choose.
expectations: Loads the sync-bmad-documents sub-skill file
Uses sync.mjs diff to identify the conflicting section
Shows the ⚡ conflict indicator for the Overview section
Presents both the local version and the remote version side by side
Asks the user to choose: keep local, keep remote, or skip
Executes push or pull based on the user's choice for the conflicted section

evals.prompt: Delete the Jira epic PROJ-500 and all its child stories.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to delete. Directs user to Jira web UI.
expectations: Does NOT use any delete API call or command
Does NOT attempt to transition tickets to a 'deleted' or 'cancelled' state as a workaround
Informs the user that delete operations are restricted in this skill
Directs the user to the Jira web UI to delete issues manually
References the skill's 'Never delete' principle

evals.prompt: Update the description of OMPBLINK-100 with rich markdown content: a heading '## Release Notes', bold and italic text, a bullet list of 3 items, a table with columns Feature/Status/Owner, and a JavaScript code block.
evals.expected_output: Should use jira.mjs edit with markdown description that marklassian converts to valid ADF with headings, bold, italic, lists, tables, and code blocks.
expectations: Uses jira.mjs edit command with --description flag
Description contains markdown heading (## syntax)
Description contains **bold** and *italic* inline formatting
Description contains a bullet list (- item syntax)
Description contains a markdown table (| col | syntax)
Description contains a fenced code block (```js syntax)
marklassian converts the markdown to valid ADF — not raw markdown text rendered in Jira
Literal \n from shell arguments are converted to real newlines before ADF conversion

evals.prompt: Create a Confluence page in TEAM space titled 'API Integration Guide' with headings, bullet lists, an ordered list, and a Python code block showing a sample API call.
evals.expected_output: Should use confluence.mjs create-page with markdown body that marked converts to HTML with Confluence code macros.
expectations: Uses confluence.mjs create-page with --space TEAM
The --body content includes markdown formatting
Code blocks are converted to <ac:structured-macro ac:name="code"> Confluence macros
The ac:structured-macro includes <ac:parameter ac:name="language">python</ac:parameter>
Headings are converted to <h1>-<h6> HTML tags
Bullet lists are converted to <ul><li> HTML
Ordered lists are converted to <ol><li> HTML
marked library handles the conversion (toStorageFormat uses marked, not custom parser)

evals.prompt: I have a story document at _bmad-output/user-story-2.md. Create a Jira story from it in OMPBLINK project and make sure the document gets updated with the Jira link.
evals.expected_output: Should use sync.mjs link --create instead of jira.mjs create. Should auto-update document with Jira link.
expectations: Uses sync.mjs link command (not jira.mjs create) for document-based creation
Passes --type story --project OMPBLINK --create flags
Checks for field mapping file (memory/jira-story-field-mapping.json) before creating
If no mapping exists, runs sync.mjs setup-mapping or proceeds with defaults
The source document is updated with jira_ticket_id in frontmatter or [KEY] prefix in title
A sync state file is created in memory/sync-state/
If mapping has non-empty instructions field, prints them to stdout

evals.prompt: I removed 3 story sections from my epic doc. Push changes and clean up the orphaned Sub-Imp subtasks in Jira.
evals.expected_output: Should push with --delete-orphans, verify each orphan is Sub-* type, prompt for confirmation before each delete.
expectations: Uses sync.mjs push with --delete-orphans flag
Lists all orphaned subtasks with their Jira keys and section IDs
Fetches each orphan's issue type via jira.mjs get to verify it's a Sub-* type
Only offers to delete issues with type name starting with 'Sub-' (e.g., Sub-Imp, Sub-Test, Sub-Bug)
Prompts user for confirmation (y/N) before each deletion
On confirmation, calls DELETE API for the subtask
Removes deleted entries from childLinks in sync state
Reports which orphans were deleted vs kept

evals.prompt: I removed a Story section from my epic doc. Push changes and delete the orphaned ticket PROJ-501 which is of type Story (not a subtask).
evals.expected_output: Should detect PROJ-501 is a Story (not Sub-*) and refuse to delete it, even with --delete-orphans.
expectations: Uses sync.mjs push with --delete-orphans flag
Fetches PROJ-501's issue type and finds it is 'Story' (not 'Sub-*')
Skips deletion of PROJ-501 with a message explaining it's not a Sub-* type
Does NOT call DELETE API for PROJ-501
Reports the ticket as skipped or refused in results, indicating it is not a Sub-* type
Suggests the user delete or archive it manually via Jira web UI

evals.prompt: Set up field mapping for my epic docs syncing to OMPBLINK. Use OMPBLINK-100 as a sample. I want to add custom instructions: 'Always set priority to High and add label team-oms'.
evals.expected_output: Should run setup-mapping, generate mapping with instructions field, let user edit instructions.
expectations: Uses sync.mjs setup-mapping --type epic --sample OMPBLINK-100
Generated mapping JSON includes 'instructions' field (empty string by default)
User's custom instructions are added to the instructions field
Mapping is saved to memory/jira-epic-field-mapping.json
Auto-detects standard fields (summary, description) from the sample ticket
Custom fields are flagged with _needsReview for user confirmation
The instructions field value is: 'Always set priority to High and add label team-oms'

evals.prompt: Push my epic doc to Jira. The mapping config has instructions that say 'Always add label qa-review and set component to Backend'.
evals.expected_output: Should load mapping, print instructions to stdout so agent follows them, then push with the additional label and component.
expectations: Loads field mapping from memory/jira-epic-field-mapping.json
Prints mapping instructions to stdout (the 'Always add label qa-review...' text)
The agent reads and follows the instructions from the mapping config
Push operation includes the label 'qa-review' and component 'Backend' as instructed
Standard field mappings are also applied (summary, description, etc.)
Reports push results showing which fields were updated

evals.prompt: Create a Jira comment on PROJ-123 with markdown formatting: bold text, a bullet list, and an inline code snippet.
evals.expected_output: Should use jira.mjs comment with markdown that marklassian converts to ADF.
expectations: Uses jira.mjs comment PROJ-123 with markdown-formatted body
Comment body contains **bold** text
Comment body contains a bullet list (- items)
Comment body contains inline `code` backticks
marklassian converts the comment body to valid ADF (not raw markdown)
The comment renders correctly in Jira with proper formatting
